0.reason: `fgetc` returns an `int`, but assigning it to `char` may lose the `EOF` value (typically -1) if `char` is unsigned, causing incorrect EOF detection and potential infinite loops.

93.reason: The function uses `isalpha(ch) || isdigit(ch) || ch == '_'` for both identifiers and integers. When processing integers, it should only consume digits (`isdigit(ch)`), otherwise it will incorrectly merge letters into integer tokens (e.g., `123abc` becomes a single token instead of stopping at `3`).
